questions.q: What is the process by which electrons are released from the hot cathode of an electron gun?
questions.explanation: Heating the metal filament gives surface electrons enough thermal energy to escape — this is thermionic emission ("thermal" + "emission"). The marker wants the correct term, not just "electrons come off when it's hot".

questions.q: In an electron gun the exit speed of the electrons is set by which quantity?
options: The strength of any magnetic field present
The diameter of the hole in the anode
questions.explanation: From \(qV = \tfrac{1}{2}mv^2\), \(v = \sqrt{2qV/m}\). Since \(q\) and \(m\) are fixed constants of the electron, the only controllable quantity is \(V\). Turning up the voltage gives faster electrons — a known, tunable speed.

questions.q: Why can only the electric field — not a magnetic field — accelerate the electrons in an electron gun?
options: Magnetic fields are too weak in a gun
Only the electric field does work on the charge; the magnetic force is perpendicular to velocity and does no work
Magnetic fields only act on positive charges
The electrons move too slowly for a magnetic field to act
questions.explanation: Only an electric field does work and so changes speed. The magnetic force is always perpendicular to the velocity, does no work, and can only steer or focus a beam — it can never be what gives the electrons their speed. This is the single most-penalised error in the topic.

questions.q: An electron is accelerated from rest through 100 V. What is its approximate exit speed? (\(m_e = 9.1\times10^{-31}\ \mathrm{kg}\), \(e = 1.6\times10^{-19}\ \mathrm{C}\))
questions.explanation: \(W = qV = 1.6\times10^{-17}\ \mathrm{J}\), then \(v = \sqrt{2W/m} = \sqrt{2(1.6\times10^{-17})/9.1\times10^{-31}} \approx 5.9\times10^{6}\ \mathrm{m\,s^{-1}}\) — about 2% of \(c\). Option D is the energy, not a speed.

questions.q: The three stages of a mass spectrometer follow the mnemonic A-S-D. What are they, in order?
questions.explanation: Accelerate (electric field gives the ions kinetic energy), Select (crossed fields pass only one speed), Deflect (a magnetic field bends ions on circles by mass). The fields are: electric, both, magnetic.

questions.q: In a velocity selector, only ions with a particular speed pass straight through. That speed is given by:
questions.explanation: Setting the electric force \(qE\) equal and opposite to the magnetic force \(qvB\), the charge cancels and \(v = E/B\). Faster ions have a larger magnetic force and are deflected one way; slower ions have a larger electric force and are deflected the other way.

questions.q: Does the velocity selector separate isotopes by mass?
options: Yes, heavier ions pass and lighter ions are blocked
No — it selects by speed only; mass and charge do not appear in \(v = E/B\)
Yes, it sorts ions by both speed and mass
No — it selects by charge only
questions.explanation: The pass condition \(v = E/B\) contains neither mass nor charge, so any ion at that speed passes regardless of mass. The selector's job is to remove speed as a variable, guaranteeing every ion enters the deflection stage at the same speed. Mass separation happens in the magnetic deflection chamber.

questions.q: In the deflection chamber, with all ions at the same speed and charge in the same field, the radius \(r = mv/qB\) means:
options: The radius depends only on the charge
The radius depends only on the mass, so heavier isotopes follow a larger-radius path
All isotopes follow the same radius
Lighter isotopes follow a larger-radius path
questions.explanation: With \(v\), \(q\) and \(B\) the same for all ions, only \(m\) varies, so \(r \propto m\). Heavier isotopes swing on a bigger circle and land further out on the detector; the position identifies the mass and the number of hits gives relative abundance.

questions.q: Singly ionised neon-20 (\(m = 3.32\times10^{-26}\ \mathrm{kg}\)) is accelerated through 1000 V. What is its approximate speed? (\(q = 1.6\times10^{-19}\ \mathrm{C}\))
questions.explanation: \(v = \sqrt{2qV/m} = \sqrt{2(1.6\times10^{-19})(1000)/3.32\times10^{-26}} \approx 9.8\times10^{4}\ \mathrm{m\,s^{-1}}\). It is far slower than an electron through 100 V because the neon ion is tens of thousands of times heavier.

questions.q: A student writes: "In the deflection chamber the magnetic field accelerates the ions and bends them into a circle." What is the error?
options: The ions are not charged, so no magnetic force acts
A magnetic field does no work, so it cannot speed the ions up — it only changes their direction
The magnetic field slows the ions down
There is no magnetic field in the deflection chamber
questions.explanation: The magnetic force is perpendicular to the velocity, so it does no work and cannot change speed. It provides the centripetal force that bends the ions into a circle at constant speed. "Accelerates" in the sense of speeding up is the most-penalised error in the module.
